name: The Wellness Ritualist
description: Travel is organized around the body. Every trip includes a spa component, yoga, meditation, or detox. Chooses destinations by wellness offering first, everything else second. Deeply into Wellness and Atmosphere (calm, intentional spaces). Food is functional and clean — plant-based, organic, no alcohol. May come across as rigid but is actually seeking transformation through physical practice.

contradictions.stated: Seeks stillness and simplicity in travel
contradictions.revealed: Actually has very exacting standards — the spa must be a specific quality, the yoga teacher must be legitimate, the food must meet dietary requirements
contradictions.expectedResolution: High-end wellness properties that combine genuine practice with luxury execution — Six Senses, Sha Wellness, Kamalaya

voiceAnswerGuidance.memorable-stays: Describes wellness-specific experiences. 'Kamalaya in Koh Samui — I did a 7-day detox and by day 4, the Thai massage therapist could tell my shoulders had released. The food was extraordinary, all from the garden.' Mentions the wellness program before the room.
voiceAnswerGuidance.anti-stay: A party hotel with no wellness facilities, loud music by the pool, heavy greasy food, and artificial everything. 'The spa was a closet with a massage table and a candle from Target.'
voiceAnswerGuidance.go-back-place: A specific wellness retreat. Describes the daily ritual — morning yoga, treatment, healthy lunch, afternoon meditation, early bed. The rhythm matters more than the place.
voiceAnswerGuidance.cultural-engagement: Through wellness traditions — Ayurveda in India, onsen culture in Japan, hammam in Morocco. Cultural immersion via the body.
voiceAnswerGuidance.emotional-core: Travels to reset, transform, and reconnect with the body. TRANSFORMATION_SEEKER.
